groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Subject, brand, page type, goal, audience, must-answer questions, facts, depth, language.

groups.title: Question Architecture
groups.description: Scope, real questions, count, categories, journey stages, objections, exclusions.

groups.title: Brand, Voice & Conversion
groups.description: Voice, tone, terminology, wording bans, and one CTA.

groups.title: SEO & Publishing
groups.description: Keywords, intent, metadata notes, structured data, format.

groups.title: Accuracy, Policies & Edge Cases
groups.description: Regulated topics, policies, sources, privacy, claim bans, escalation.

variables.label: FAQ subject
variables.placeholder: Creatornivo subscriptions and billing
variables.hint: Name the product, service, company, policy, or topic this FAQ covers.

help.why: It defines the central subject and keeps every question within scope.
help.avoid: Several unrelated products on one FAQ page.

variables.label: Brand or organization
variables.placeholder: Creatornivo
variables.hint: Enter the name readers should associate with the answers.

help.why: It ensures consistent naming and prevents the model from inventing an organization.
help.avoid: Inventing a brand name or multiple conflicting entities.

variables.label: FAQ page type
variables.placeholder: Choose the closest page type
variables.hint: Select the context so the question structure matches the reader’s needs.

help.why: It changes the native question architecture, answer emphasis, and escalation logic.
help.avoid: Picking a type that does not match the real page purpose.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Choose the main outcome
variables.hint: Choose the single most important job this FAQ should perform.

help.why: It determines which questions deserve priority and how the page should guide readers.
help.avoid: Stacking sell + SEO + support as equal primary goals.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Who will read this page, and what do they already know?
variables.hint: Describe the main reader, their context, and likely level of familiarity.

help.why: It controls vocabulary, assumed knowledge, question selection, and answer depth.
help.example: Who will read this page, and what do they already know?
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Must-answer questions
variables.placeholder: List the questions the final page must answer.
variables.hint: Add exact questions or topics that cannot be omitted.

help.why: It captures the user’s actual information needs instead of relying only on model inference.
help.example: How do Free quotas work? What is included in Pro?
help.avoid: Leaving out non-negotiable policy questions.

variables.label: Confirmed facts and answers
variables.placeholder: Provide approved features, prices, steps, limits, dates, policies, or answer notes.
variables.hint: Include only information approved for publication; unsupported details will be omitted.

help.why: It supplies the factual basis required to answer questions without fabrication.
help.example: Free: 5/day UTC. Pro: 100/month. No invented testimonials.
help.avoid: Unverified prices, guarantees, or features not live yet.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Choose a language
variables.hint: The complete FAQ package will be written in this language.

help.why: It controls the language of all public copy, metadata, and implementation notes.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Answer depth
variables.placeholder: Choose answer depth
variables.hint: Sets the typical detail level while allowing complex answers to be longer.

help.why: It materially changes readability, page length, and the amount of explanation.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Overall page length
variables.placeholder: Choose a target
variables.hint: Auto selects a useful length from the scope, audience, and question count.

help.why: It lets users control scope without requiring a technical word-count decision.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Page introduction
variables.placeholder: Choose whether to include an intro
variables.hint: A short introduction is used only when it helps orient the reader.

help.why: It controls a visible page element that may help complex pages but burden simple support FAQs.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Additional context
variables.placeholder: Add any context or requirements not covered above.
variables.hint: Use this for one-off constraints; leave it blank when unnecessary.

help.why: It provides one controlled place for important exceptions without creating miscellaneous fields.
help.example: Add any context or requirements not covered above.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Scope boundaries
variables.placeholder: What is included, and where should the page stop?
variables.hint: Define boundaries when the subject could easily become too broad.

help.why: It prevents irrelevant questions and keeps the page focused.
help.example: What is included, and where should the page stop?
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Existing customer questions
variables.placeholder: Paste questions from support tickets, sales calls, comments, or search data.
variables.hint: Real customer wording should guide phrasing when available.

help.why: It improves relevance and preserves authentic user language.
help.example: Paste questions from support tickets, sales calls, comments, or search data.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Target question count
variables.placeholder: Choose a range
variables.hint: Auto selects enough questions to cover the topic without padding.

help.why: It controls page breadth and prevents either thin or unnecessarily long output.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Question categories
variables.placeholder: Getting started, Billing, Security, Troubleshooting
variables.hint: Suggest category names or leave blank for automatic grouping.

help.why: It allows the output to align with an existing website or help-center structure.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Reader journey stages
variables.placeholder:
Discovering
Comparing options
Before choosing
Getting started
Using the product
Troubleshooting
Renewing or upgrading
Leaving or cancelling
variables.hint: Choose where readers are in their journey; select only stages that matter. Select all that apply (one per line). Options: Discovering; Comparing options; Before choosing; Getting started; Using the product; Troubleshooting; Renewing or upgrading; Leaving or cancelling.

help.why: It helps order questions according to when readers encounter uncertainty.
help.example:
Before choosing
Getting started
Using the product
help.avoid: Selecting every stage when only two apply.

variables.defaultValue:
Before choosing
Getting started
Using the product

variables.label: Objections to address
variables.placeholder:
Price
Time or effort
Fit or eligibility
Trust or credibility
Privacy or security
Setup difficulty
Support availability
Cancellation or refunds
No objections
variables.hint: Include only objections that genuinely apply to the offer or topic. Select all that apply (one per line). Options: Price; Time or effort; Fit or eligibility; Trust or credibility; Privacy or security; Setup difficulty; Support availability; Cancellation or refunds; No objections.

help.why: It helps resolve real decision barriers without manufacturing fear or resistance.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Topics to exclude
variables.placeholder: List questions, subjects, or comparisons that must not appear.
variables.hint: Use this to prevent overlap, sensitive disclosure, or off-topic content.

help.why: It establishes negative scope and reduces accidental disclosure or duplication.
help.example: List questions, subjects, or comparisons that must not appear.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Brand voice
variables.placeholder: Calm, direct, friendly, and technically credible
variables.hint: Describe the established voice, or leave blank for clear and natural wording.

help.why: It aligns the page with the brand without requiring multiple overlapping style controls.
help.example: Calm, direct, friendly, and technically credible
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Answer tone
variables.placeholder: Choose a tone
variables.hint: Select the dominant tone; factual accuracy always takes priority.

help.why: It changes how direct, warm, formal, reassuring, or technical the answers feel.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Preferred terminology
variables.placeholder: Terms, capitalization, product names, or wording to use consistently.
variables.hint: Add approved terminology that readers should see.

help.why: It prevents inconsistent product naming and terminology.
help.example: Terms, capitalization, product names, or wording to use consistently.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Wording to avoid
variables.placeholder: List phrases, labels, claims, or tones that should not appear.
variables.hint: Use this for brand-sensitive wording beyond factual restrictions.

help.why: It protects brand positioning where specific phrasing is unacceptable.
help.example: List phrases, labels, claims, or tones that should not appear.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: FAQ call to action
variables.placeholder: Choose the next step
variables.hint: Use one proportional next step after the FAQ, or omit it when unnecessary.

help.why: It controls the reader’s next step without forcing a promotional ending.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Preferred CTA wording
variables.placeholder: Contact support
variables.hint: Add exact button or link wording, or leave blank for a natural suggestion.

help.why: It allows exact interface wording when an approved CTA label already exists.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: CTA destination
variables.placeholder: `https://example.com/support`
variables.hint: Add the destination page; leave blank when no link is available.

help.why: It prevents invented links and makes the final CTA implementation-ready.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Primary search phrase
variables.placeholder: Creatornivo billing FAQ
variables.hint: Use only when a specific search phrase matters; never force repetition.

help.why: It supports search-intent alignment when the user has an established target phrase.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Related search phrases
variables.placeholder: Add closely related phrases, questions, or named entities.
variables.hint: Use these for natural coverage, not keyword stuffing.

help.why: It helps cover adjacent search language without requiring many separate keyword fields.
help.example: Add closely related phrases, questions, or named entities.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Search intent
variables.placeholder: Choose the dominant intent
variables.hint: Auto infers intent from the page goal, questions, and page type.

help.why: It influences question order, metadata, and how directly answers address the searcher’s task.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: SEO title notes
variables.placeholder: Required wording or title preference
variables.hint: Add words that must appear in the suggested SEO title.

help.why: It captures title-specific constraints without asking the user to write the complete title.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Meta description notes
variables.placeholder: Value point or wording to emphasize
variables.hint: Add optional direction for the suggested search description.

help.why: It allows control over the snippet’s emphasis when that message materially matters.
help.example: Value point or wording to emphasize
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Structured-data output
variables.placeholder: Choose an output mode
variables.hint: Generate implementation guidance or JSON-LD only when appropriate.

help.why: It prevents the system from assuming every FAQ page needs generated markup.
help.avoid: Promising FAQ rich results or inventing page URLs.

variables.label: Canonical page URL
variables.placeholder: `https://example.com/faq`
variables.hint: Used only when a final public page address exists.

help.why: It supports accurate implementation notes without inventing a page address.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Publishing format
variables.placeholder: Choose the preferred format
variables.hint: Select the format best suited to the site’s publishing workflow.

help.why: It changes heading, accordion, link, and code presentation for implementation.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Sensitive topic area
variables.placeholder: Choose when relevant
variables.hint: Select a regulated or high-stakes area so answers use appropriate caution.

help.why: It activates subject-specific safeguards without burdening ordinary FAQ pages.
help.avoid: Medical/legal claims without approved policy wording.

variables.label: Relevant jurisdiction
variables.placeholder: European Union, California, or Ukraine
variables.hint: Add the country, state, or region when rules depend on location.

help.why: It prevents location-dependent requirements from being presented as universal.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Approved policies or terms
variables.placeholder: Paste approved refund, privacy, delivery, eligibility, or support rules.
variables.hint: Use exact current policy information when answers must reflect formal terms.

help.why: It grounds policy answers in approved language and reduces contradictions with formal pages.
help.example: Paste approved refund, privacy, delivery, eligibility, or support rules.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Sources and reference links
variables.placeholder: Paste source links, document titles, or reference notes.
variables.hint: Sources guide accuracy; unavailable material must not be presented as checked.

help.why: It supplies traceable support for factual or high-stakes answers.
help.example: Paste source links, document titles, or reference notes.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Details likely to change
variables.placeholder: Prices, availability, deadlines, feature limits, laws, or schedules.
variables.hint: Mark details that need cautious wording or pre-publication verification.

help.why: It prevents volatile information from being presented as permanently fixed.
help.example: Prices, availability, deadlines, feature limits, laws, or schedules.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Privacy restrictions
variables.placeholder: Information that must not be disclosed or requested from readers.
variables.hint: Exclude personal, confidential, internal, or security-sensitive details.

help.why: It reduces privacy, confidentiality, and security risks in public-facing answers.
help.example: Information that must not be disclosed or requested from readers.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Claims and promises to avoid
variables.placeholder: Unsupported guarantees, comparisons, certifications, or outcomes.
variables.hint: Add restrictions beyond the template’s permanent truthfulness rules.

help.why: It captures brand- or industry-specific claim boundaries.
help.example: Unsupported guarantees, comparisons, certifications, or outcomes.
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.

variables.label: Support escalation route
variables.placeholder: When and how should readers contact support or another responsible team?
variables.hint: Provide the approved next step for cases the FAQ cannot safely resolve.

help.why: It enables a safe handoff for account-specific, disputed, or unresolved situations.
help.example: When and how should readers contact support or another responsible team?
help.avoid: Invented features, prices, policies, reviews, or unsupported guarantees.
